concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Ways to store graph data, typically using adjacency lists or matrices.

concepts.name: Weighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have associated numerical values.

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: The path between two vertices in a graph with the minimum total edge weight.

concepts.name: Dijkstra Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm to find the shortest paths from a single source vertex to all other vertices in a weighted graph with non-negative edge weights.

concepts.name: Priority Queue
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that stores elements with associated priorities and allows efficient retrieval of the element with the highest (or lowest) priority.

concepts.name: Distance Array
concepts.short_definition: An array storing the shortest distance found so far from the source vertex to each other vertex.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection of vertices for which the shortest path from the source has been finalized.

concepts.name: Relaxation
concepts.short_definition: The process of updating the shortest distance to a vertex if a shorter path is found through an adjacent vertex.

concepts.name: Greedy Approach
concepts.short_definition: An algorithmic paradigm that makes the locally optimal choice at each stage with the hope of finding a global optimum.

concepts.name: Non-negative Weights
concepts.short_definition: A condition for Dijkstra's algorithm where all edge weights must be zero or positive.
